key_points: Mughal decline led to decentralization and rise of autonomous regional states
European companies used military power, alliances, and political strategies to expand influence
Political instability affected taxation, trade, and governance in local societies
Alliances between regional powers and Europeans were strategic but often temporary
Battles like Plassey and Buxar marked the beginning of British territorial expansion in India
examples: British East India Company gained revenue collection rights in Bengal after Plassey
Marathas formed alliances with local rulers to counter the Nizam of Hyderabad
Changes in rulers’ policies affected farmers, traders, and townspeople
French East India Company lost influence due to British military victories
formulas: Political Changes → Impact on society + economy + governance
European Expansion → Decline of regional autonomy + British dominance
Major Battles → Shifts in territorial and political control
exam_tips: Focus on cause-and-effect of Mughal decline on regional powers
Remember European companies’ strategies and their consequences
Be able to explain social and economic impacts of political changes
Use battles and alliances as examples to strengthen answers
Connect regional politics with the broader story of British colonial expansion
